title: will AI overtake banking in the near future?

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: will AI overtake banking in the near future?

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text: As an AI language model, I cannot predict the future, but it is possible that AI and automation will continue to change the face of banking. AI-powered chatbots and machine learning algorithms are already being used to improve customer service, fraud detection, and risk management. In the future, AI may also play a bigger role in underwriting loans, streamlining back-office operations, and creating more personalized investment strategies. However, it is unlikely that AI will completely replace human bankers, as there will always be a need for human insight and decision-making in complex financial situations.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: what jobs are predicted to be overtake by ai

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
Artificial intelligence and automation are expected to impact many jobs across various industries. Some of the jobs that may be overtaken by AI in the near future include:

1. Data Entry Clerks: With the advancement of AI-powered bots and automation software, data entry work can be done automatically with much higher speed and accuracy than humans.

2. Telemarketers: Many companies are using chatbots and automated software to reach out and interact with customers, reducing the need for human telemarketers.

3. Cashiers: Self-service checkouts and automated payment systems are becoming more popular in retail, reducing the need for human cashiers.

4. Bookkeepers and Accounting Clerks: AI and automation are being used to automate bookkeeping tasks such as data entry, record keeping, and reconciling accounts.

5. Assembly Line Workers: Robotics and automation technology are replacing humans in many assembly line jobs as they are better suited for repetitive and precise tasks.

However, it is important to note that while AI and automation may replace certain tasks and jobs, they could also create new job opportunities in areas such as data analysis, software development, and IT support.
